questions.question_text: A student scored $27$ out of $30$ on a quiz. Express this as a percentage.

options.why: Divided the wrong way, computing $\dfrac{30}{27}$ instead of $\dfrac{27}{30}$.
Found the percentage of marks lost ($3$ out of $30$) instead of the percentage scored.
Divided $60$ by $45$ instead of $45$ by $60$, inverting the fraction.
Found the percentage remaining ($15$ out of $60$) rather than the $45$.
Wrote $\dfrac{60}{240}$, reversing which quantity is the base.
Computed $\dfrac{240}{60}$ but forgot to multiply by $100$.

questions.question_text: In a class of $32$ students, $30$ completed their homework. What percentage did not complete their homework?

options.why: Found the percentage who completed homework instead of those who did not.
Gave the number who did not complete ($2$) as a percentage without dividing by $32$.

questions.question_text: Express $40$ cents as a percentage of $\$8$.

options.why: Divided $40$ by $800$ correctly but forgot to multiply by $100$.

questions.question_text: In a mixture, $40$ g of Chemical A is mixed with $85$ g of Chemical B. What percentage of the mixture is Chemical A?

options.why: Used $\dfrac{40}{85}$, dividing by Chemical B alone instead of the whole mixture of $125$ g.
Found the percentage of Chemical B in the mixture rather than Chemical A.
